reviews.review_title: Tries to do too many things
reviews.review_description:
Area Man Lives is a quirky narrative that has had me laughing out loud at times. The voice acting and sound in general are clearly the best features of the game. This self-described radio drama may have been best left to another platform though. 

As a game, you are introduced to myriad functions as a radio producer, only to have them interrupted by calls and the actual plot of the game getting underway. Yes, despite what it might look like at the start of this game, very few of your tasks will actually involve using the controls of the radio station. Instead you will find some object to affect the gameplay in a way that’s often confusing and hard to predict, if not downright convoluted. The game also features voice recognition, to absolutely no effect. I could be farting into the microphone for all the difference it would make. 

I see no reason this game must be VR. It takes place entirely in a single and the actual manipulation of objects is buggy, sometimes requiring a restart to get it working again.  

I think the developers had several ideas that ended up merged into a single product. 

Worst of all, when i finally figured out the solutions in this game i was angry it the developers for thinking that somehow i was expected to think of doing this obscure thing at this particular time. I may still recommend the game if it is on sale or you are interested in following the developer (or their partner, Cyan Ventures). 

I was willing to stick with this game to the end (though the save file seems to be bugged and not display correct percentages) so it obviously had some appeal to it.  

Fun characters, fun story, fun music, frustrating experience.

I wish I would give 5 stars, unfortunately it became so frustrating to do the same things all over again. I’ve played games in which I had to go backwards but the challenges were gone, so I didn’t have to do it again. I just found out there’s a way to cut all the talk, I don’t think no one likes sitting there just listening to extensive talking between the characters and then having to hear them repetitively . I will try to finish the game if I can at least cut the talking. If it wasn’t for that it would be a great game.

developer_response.body:
Hi lenefa, 
We're sorry some aspects of the game weren't to your liking! With a game focused around being a disc jockey at a radio station, talking plays a huge part in the experience. 
We hope you'll continue and find the rest of the game enjoyable. 
Best wishes!

reviews.review_description: I would have more fun lighting a $20 bill on fire and watching it burn.

developer_response.body:
Hey LaineyLight,
We're sorry to see you don't like the game. Can you provide more details? We'd like to know what you did or didn't like. 
Best wishes, 
C.W.

reviews.review_description: I've had this game for like 2 years, and I have no clue how many endings there are and I NEED to know.

developer_response.body:
Hi NeedyNoodle! 

There are 5 branch endings in addition to the "good" ending. 
Thank you for your review - we're thrilled you've been enjoying AREA MAN LIVES!

reviews.review_description: No matter what you say the characters always have the same reaction so the one aspect I was looking forward too was just a lie. I believed that the story would shape determined by what you say but it's not. The producer is too annoying, he cuts off everyone and you can barely make out what anyone says because he needs to speak every 5 seconds. There's no tutorial explaining what anything does or how the recording works for the commercials. This game is 100% not worth playing!

developer_response.body: We're sorry you have not enjoyed the early part of the game. We hope you stick with it. Your responses to the characters do trigger different dialog in many places, but the variety of responses build the longer you play. And the actions you take in the game are what really branch the narrative as you try to direct the Area Man, sometimes to his demise. As the game moves along you'll find many more instances where both your own voice and your actions drive the story. - Amy and Ryan

reviews.review_description:
It’s hard not to admire what Area Man Lives tries to do; extensive use of voice control, a great cast, wonderful voice acting, and a story that’s equal parts Groundhog Day and 1971’s Vanishing Point! You okay a Radio DJ helping an Area Man survive to save the day. It’s great, but hampered by some technical issues and some tediously repetitive sections - all of which can be fixed with an update or two! 

For a full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Area Man Lives :)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review! We are aware that the repetition ended up being more tedious than we intended and that is one of our highest priorities at the moment! We're also aware of a few technical issues and we'll be addressing those as quickly as we can! Great Feedback!

EDIT: We've released our first patch! Hopefully this addresses some of the frustration with the repeat sections of the game, along with a few other fixes.

reviews.review_description: He won't shut up about not having kids

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review. The Producer is designed to be obnoxious at first. A guy we love to hate and then learn to love. The longer you play, the more his character arc shifts the way he interacts with you and the way he views the world. Once you get past the open sandbox phase, the Producer takes a back seat to the magical-realism adventure you get drawn into with John and the Area Man. The honking of the truck marks the end of this early phase of the game.

reviews.review_description:
I’m a couple of hours in and It really is a smart idea for a VR game and a great use of the technology 

The story so far is interesting and engaging but does feel a little disjointed because of the way the narrative works in the game.

My biggest gripe on top of the slightly choppy call and response is the clunky and inaccurate controls. My biggest struggles and reason for ending my session earlier then I wanted was the sudden inability to remove a record from the player.

There is a lot to take in when you first start the game so I felt some further explanation as to what is going on couldn’t hurt.

Promising game. Will give it another whirl soon

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write a review. The issue with the record player we'll need to look at. Thank you for letting us know about it! As you progress, we hope the mysterious things happening will become more clear.

reviews.review_description: This is one of the most interesting games ive ever played and a fantastic way to do a story game however i do want 2 things. I want the point and click to grab to be a little more consistent and for the love of all that is good and weird i want an alternate version thats the same story just from area man and John's perspective

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to write a review. It means a lot! We'll see what we can do about the point and click. When items are so close together, it's sometimes a struggle to target each, we totally get that. I'm guessing you'd want to see the visuals of what's happening to the party in the truck? Since we've built this like a radio drama, it's all been from Taylor's perspective so I love your idea! Thank you for that too!

reviews.review_title: It's such a good game but hard!!
reviews.review_description:
I would love to see a update for the game giving us players a ability to skip or simply request hints on how to solve some of the puzzles, I've been stuck on the moose in book two for almost half a year and I've done my research! No one! Not a single person! Has beat this game. Please!!! It's such a good game and it's in dire need of some help😭:'( 

Update for Ryan who responded, the printer isnt giving anything at the moment, I'm stuck at the moose in the second book and have been for months, I've tried every time, I've tried talking to the moose, yelling at it, trying to talk to huck, trying to slowly walk the car away around the moose, small things change but no real hints ! The others before this which got me invested had a method to it but I've tried everything and can't get past this part! No one has beaten your game or even gotten close and I think it's safe to say you need another tool in game for players

developer_response.body:
Potential Spoiler Alert!


Hey Sarge.Max420, I'm glad you're enjoying the game! A few hints. On replays of a scene, pay attention to how the dialogue changes.  There are usually a few hints incorporated into the character's lines.  Also, when you're back in the studio after Area Man meets his demise, try picking up items and pay attention to the printer...

-Ryan

reviews.review_description:
In a great way.  I haven't played for very long, and I haven't gotten far but the humor is fantastic. Totally laughing aloud.

You actually use your own voice, something I haven't experienced before in a VR game. It has that immediate sense of the traditional adventure/puzzle genre where you're dropped into it without handholding. 

That said, there's a tutorial to get the hang of the controls.

Can't wait to see how this shakes out!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for review! Comedy is subjective... but its good to see that some people enjoy our kind of funny! 

The Numinous Games Team

reviews.review_description: I happened to notice the song by future folk that’s just a great reference also the game was excellent

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to write a review! We're so happy you enjoyed that Future Folk song! They were incredibly gracious with us!

reviews.review_title: Enjoyed it for a bit but..
reviews.review_description:
After I had played for about an hour and a half , just about every scenario was very repetitive, like if you only owned five legos. I understand you are a very small company but repetition throws people off.

However ,what I adore about this game is it’s intricate systems ,like speaking and the characters reacting accordingly . I also loved how in the little environment they give you , there is plenty to find.

20$ is a fair price for this game unlike games like Arizona sunshine and population one 

I look foreword to seeing where this game could go but for now my review stands,thank you for reading.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to write a review! We're so happy you enjoyed our weird game for a bit! Check out our new update, it might help a bit more with the repetition!

reviews.review_description: I want my money back .

developer_response.body:
Hi mmwoody5 - we're sorry to see you did not like the game. We hope you'll give it another chance before refunding your purchase. 
You may be eligible for a refund if you used the app for less than 2 hours and you've requested a refund less than 14 days after purchase.
To return a Meta Quest app using the Oculus app:
1. From the Oculus app, tap Menu then tap Settings.
2. Tap Purchase History.
3. Select the app you want to return then tap Request Refund.
Best wishes!

reviews.review_description:
I like how this game uses your microphone to interact with the characters but you need to have better hints. I’m still stuck on the police chase section of the game.

Edit for devs: I’m gonna see if that’s a record in the radio booth and play it to maybe distract the cop. Thx for the hint!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to reach out to us! We're glad you're enjoying our game. Remember, the Police Officer really loves Knuckleback! :-)

Hopefully you've noticed that each time you fail, the fast-forward version of that section has additional hints for you. Failing oftentimes reveals some pretty interesting and humorous dialog.

reviews.review_description: The idea is good but the game doesn’t pick my voice up. I’ve reset the game used different mics and turned the mic option on and off. It also won’t let me progress everytime I get to a certain point. Could you either tell me how to fix this or let me refund the game please?

developer_response.body: Hi Known_As_Carter, we are sorry to hear about the issue with your mic! We'd love to help. Please send an email to support@cyan.com and we'll be able to assist you further!

reviews.review_description: The game o far is pretty good but I’ve had some bugs which I get stuck in the roof and have to restart the game but other than that it’s good buy

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to write a review. We are glad you are enjoying AREA MAN LIVES.

EDIT: We've released our first patch! Hopefully this addresses some of the frustration with the repeat sections of the game, along with a few other fixes including player height regardless of if you're sitting or standing.

reviews.review_description:
5 out of 5 stars. Game is so creative, fun, in depth, not too much. The story line is brilliant. Max Greenfield did an amazing job with his voice acting. Immediately noticed his voice as soon as the game started bringing his lines in. 

I can say I recommend for all ages! So fun!

developer_response.body:
Glad you loved the game. Thank you for taking the time to review this indie title. 

The Numinous Games Team

reviews.review_description:
It's so buggy and too hard
￼

developer_response.body:
Hi noll.160929, 
We're sorry to see you did not like the game. We hope you gave it a good try before giving up. It rewards repeated plays!
Cheers, 
C.W.

reviews.review_description: Such a nice game to relax and fool around in!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to try out our little game and write a review here! It's very much appreciated!
